title: Subway Signals: A Complete Guide
excerpt: Ce document également disponible en français
content_html:
Ce document également disponible en français

All divisions of the New York City subway use what is now called "wayside color-light block signalling", that is, signals are physically located to the side of the track (as opposed to being overhead, or transmitted directly to the train), utilize the colors of lights (as opposed to semaphores or other devices, or positions or shapes of lights), and rely on dividing tracks into discrete blocks (or track sections or track circuits) to electrically detect the presence of trains. Nearly all of the routes of the current subway are signalled with classic wayside block signalling, whose principles have not changed markedly since the opening of the IRT in 1904. (In-cab signalling and recent position- and radio-based technologies are slowly being deployed on some lines; the first to be cut over is the "L", chosen because it shares no trackage with other routes, and has a relatively simple service pattern, i.e. no express service.)

Tracks are divided into track circuits (or sections, or blocks), normally hundreds of feet long, by insulated joints in the rails. Trains' wheels and axles short-circuit the two rails to indicate their presence in a block. While every signal occurs at an insulated joint (a block boundary), not every joint has a signal at it. Each block can either be occupied (any portion of a train in any part of it) or vacant (no train anywhere) at any time. Every signal has a control length, defined as the distance, a number of whole blocks beyond the signal (i.e., in the direction of traffic governed by it), whose occupancy forces the signal to red ("stop") - the control length of a signal enforces that separation between trains. A signal being clear (green or yellow) attests that there are no trains, or parts of trains, or other obstructions anywhere in its control length, that is, that its control length is clear of dangers. The New York City subway relies on automatic train stops to "trip" noncompliant trains, that is, force them to obey signals that indicate "stop". Control lengths and signal placements are chosen such that an out-of-control train "tripped" by a train stop will come to a halt before reaching the obstruction or danger. Control lengths also overlap each other to keep trains at least one signal apart.

Signals in the vicinity of switches ("switch-tracks", "points" (UK)) must be and are controlled in a coordinated fashion known as interlocking, which usually involves a human tower operator selecting the routings of trains.

The pages listed below explain more about the various kinds of signals and signal features, and include illustrations of the signals' aspects (distinctive displays of colored lights) and the corresponding indications (what they mean). The discussions are oriented towards those who have spent some time looking at the system from the front of a train, and have already seen, if not fully understood, the signal system's appearances and behaviors. They should be browsed as a set.

Approach, Automatic, and Marker Signals
The most common signals, plus train order signals and miscellaneous light signals.

Train Stops
The mechanism that forcibly stops trains that disregard signal indications.

Time Signals
Grade time and station time signals that enforce train speed limits.

Interlocking
How signals and switches are controlled together.

Home Signals
The signals with two or more "heads" near switches.

Sign Signals
Fixed metal signs used in signalling.

Holdout Signals and Bidirectional Traffic
Signals that enforce the direction of traffic on a length of track.

Single-Line Signal Diagrams
How to understand the "single line" diagram format used to express the layout and capability of signals.

NXSYS, Signalling and Interlocking Simulator
A complete interactive simulation of virtually all features of New York City Subway signaling.

Page Credits

Descriptions and graphics in this section (unless otherwise noted) are Copyright © 1997-2002 Bernard S. Greenberg (contact).

blocks.html_en: All divisions of the New York City subway use what is now called "wayside color-light block signalling", that is, signals are physically located to the side of the track (as opposed to being overhead, or transmitted directly to the train), utilize the colors of lights (as opposed to semaphores or other devices, or positions or shapes of lights), and rely on dividing tracks into discrete blocks (or track sections or track circuits) to electrically detect the presence of trains. (In-cab signalling and recent position- and radio-based technologies are slowly being deployed on some lines; the first to be cut over is the "L", chosen because it shares no trackage with other routes, and has a relatively simple service pattern, i.e. no express service.)

sentences.en: All divisions of the New York City subway use what is now called "wayside color-light block signalling", that is, signals are physically located to the side of the track (as opposed to being overhead, or transmitted directly to the train), utilize the colors of lights (as opposed to semaphores or other devices, or positions or shapes of lights), and rely on dividing tracks into discrete blocks (or track sections or track circuits) to electrically detect the presence of trains.
sentences.zh: 纽约市地铁的所有部门都使用现在所谓的“路边色光块信号”，即信号物理上位于轨道侧面（而不是在头顶上，或直接传输到火车），利用灯光的颜色（而不是信号量或其他设备，或灯光的位置或形状），并依靠将轨道划分为离散的块（或轨道部分或轨道电路）来以电气方式检测火车的存在。

sentences.en: Nearly all of the routes of the current subway are signalled with classic wayside block signalling, whose principles have not changed markedly since the opening of the IRT in 1904.
sentences.zh: 当前地铁的几乎所有路线均采用经典的路边街区信号灯，其原理自 1904 年 IRT 开通以来一直没有明显改变。

sentences.en: (In-cab signalling and recent position- and radio-based technologies are slowly being deployed on some lines; the first to be cut over is the "L", chosen because it shares no trackage with other routes, and has a relatively simple service pattern, i.e. no express service.)
sentences.zh: （驾驶室信号以及最新的基于位置和无线电的技术正在慢慢地在一些线路上部署；第一个被切换的是“L”，选择它是因为它不与其他线路共享轨道，并且服务模式相对简单，即没有快车服务。）

blocks.html_en: Tracks are divided into track circuits (or sections, or blocks), normally hundreds of feet long, by insulated joints in the rails. Trains' wheels and axles short-circuit the two rails to indicate their presence in a block. While every signal occurs at an insulated joint (a block boundary), not every joint has a signal at it. Each block can either be occupied (any portion of a train in any part of it) or vacant (no train anywhere) at any time. Every signal has a control length, defined as the distance, a number of whole blocks beyond the signal (i.e., in the direction of traffic governed by it), whose occupancy forces the signal to red ("stop") - the control length of a signal enforces that separation between trains. A signal being clear (green or yellow) attests that there are no trains, or parts of trains, or other obstructions anywhere in its control length, that is, that its control length is clear of dangers. The New York City subway relies on automatic train stops to "trip" noncompliant trains, that is, force them to obey signals that indicate "stop". Control lengths and signal placements are chosen such that an out-of-control train "tripped" by a train stop will come to a halt before reaching the obstruction or danger. Control lengths also overlap each other to keep trains at least one signal apart.

sentences.en: Tracks are divided into track circuits (or sections, or blocks), normally hundreds of feet long, by insulated joints in the rails.
sentences.zh: 轨道通过铁轨中的绝缘接头分为轨道电路（或部分或块），通常有数百英尺长。

sentences.en: Trains' wheels and axles short-circuit the two rails to indicate their presence in a block.
sentences.zh: 火车的车轮和车轴使两条铁轨短路，以表明它们在一个街区中的存在。

sentences.en: While every signal occurs at an insulated joint (a block boundary), not every joint has a signal at it.
sentences.zh: 虽然每个信号都出现在绝缘接头（块边界）处，但并非每个接头处都有信号。

sentences.en: Each block can either be occupied (any portion of a train in any part of it) or vacant (no train anywhere) at any time.
sentences.zh: 每个区块可以随时被占用（火车的任何部分）或空置（任何地方都没有火车）。

sentences.en: Every signal has a control length, defined as the distance, a number of whole blocks beyond the signal (i.e., in the direction of traffic governed by it), whose occupancy forces the signal to red ("stop") - the control length of a signal enforces that separation between trains.
sentences.zh: 每个信号灯都有一个控制长度，定义为距离，信号灯之外的整个街区的数量（即，在其管辖的交通方向上），其占用迫使信号灯变红（“停止”）——信号灯的控制长度强制列车之间的间隔。

sentences.en: A signal being clear (green or yellow) attests that there are no trains, or parts of trains, or other obstructions anywhere in its control length, that is, that its control length is clear of dangers.
sentences.zh: 信号灯清晰（绿色或黄色）证明在其控制长度内任何地方都没有火车、火车的一部分或其他障碍物，也就是说，其控制长度没有危险。

sentences.en: The New York City subway relies on automatic train stops to "trip" noncompliant trains, that is, force them to obey signals that indicate "stop".
sentences.zh: 纽约市地铁依靠自动车站来“绊倒”不合规的列车，即迫使它们服从指示“停车”的信号。

sentences.en: Control lengths and signal placements are chosen such that an out-of-control train "tripped" by a train stop will come to a halt before reaching the obstruction or danger.
sentences.zh: 选择控制长度和信号布局，使得失控的火车被车站“绊倒”，在到达障碍物或危险之前停下来。

sentences.en: Control lengths also overlap each other to keep trains at least one signal apart.
sentences.zh: 控制长度也相互重叠，以使列车至少间隔一个信号。

The discussions are oriented towards those who have spent some time looking at the system from the front of a train, and have already seen, if not fully understood, the signal system's appearances and behaviors. They should be browsed as a set.

sentences.en: The discussions are oriented towards those who have spent some time looking at the system from the front of a train, and have already seen, if not fully understood, the signal system's appearances and behaviors.

content_html_zh:
该文件还有法文版

纽约市地铁的所有部门都使用现在所谓的“路边色光块信号”，即信号物理上位于轨道侧面（而不是在头顶上，或直接传输到火车），利用灯光的颜色（而不是信号量或其他设备，或灯光的位置或形状），并依靠将轨道划分为离散的块（或轨道部分或轨道电路）来以电气方式检测火车的存在。 当前地铁的几乎所有路线均采用经典的路边街区信号灯，其原理自 1904 年 IRT 开通以来一直没有明显改变。 （驾驶室信号以及最新的基于位置和无线电的技术正在慢慢地在一些线路上部署；第一个被切换的是“L”，选择它是因为它不与其他线路共享轨道，并且服务模式相对简单，即没有快车服务。）

轨道通过铁轨中的绝缘接头分为轨道电路（或部分或块），通常有数百英尺长。 火车的车轮和车轴使两条铁轨短路，以表明它们在一个街区中的存在。 虽然每个信号都出现在绝缘接头（块边界）处，但并非每个接头处都有信号。 每个区块可以随时被占用（火车的任何部分）或空置（任何地方都没有火车）。 每个信号灯都有一个控制长度，定义为距离，信号灯之外的整个街区的数量（即，在其管辖的交通方向上），其占用迫使信号灯变红（“停止”）——信号灯的控制长度强制列车之间的间隔。 信号灯清晰（绿色或黄色）证明在其控制长度内任何地方都没有火车、火车的一部分或其他障碍物，也就是说，其控制长度没有危险。 纽约市地铁依靠自动车站来“绊倒”不合规的列车，即迫使它们服从指示“停车”的信号。 选择控制长度和信号布局，使得失控的火车被车站“绊倒”，在到达障碍物或危险之前停下来。 控制长度也相互重叠，以使列车至少间隔一个信号。

道岔（“道岔轨道”、“点”（英国））附近的信号必须以一种称为联锁的协调方式进行控制，这通常涉及塔操作员选择火车路线。

下面列出的页面详细解释了各种信号和信号特征，并包括信号方面的插图（独特的彩色灯光显示）和相应的指示（它们的含义）。 这些讨论面向那些花了一些时间从火车前部观察该系统，并且已经了解（如果没有完全理解）信号系统的外观和行为的人。 它们应该作为一个集合来浏览。

进场信号、自动信号和标志信号 最常见的信号，以及列车指令信号和其他灯光信号。

列车停止 强制停止无视信号指示的列车的机制。

时间信号 对时间和车站时间信号进行分级，以强制执行列车速度限制。

联锁 信号和开关如何一起控制。

主信号 开关附近有两个或多个“头”的信号。

标志信号 用于信号传输的固定金属标志。

强制信号灯和双向交通信号灯在一定长度的轨道上强制交通方向。

单线信号图 如何理解用于表达信号布局和功能的“单线”图格式。

NXSYS，信号和联锁模拟器 纽约地铁信号几乎所有功能的完整交互式模拟。

页面学分
